insight: BottyMcBotFace is over 100 rating points stronger than Master Bot in casual match play. It uses Master Bot's engine but augments it with a large opening book of strong rushes, and employs alpha-beta pruning to decide between rush and normal Master Bot builds.
author: elyot
context: Developer elyot investigated casual match bot matchmaking quality after player complaints about being matched with weak bots.

insight: Among casual match bots, the strongest tier includes BottyMcBotFace and variants of Master Bot with adjusted opening books. Bots like 'deep_drone' and 'Masterbot2016' were considered the best of the remaining casual bots, with Masterbot2016 being roughly comparable to modern ExpertBot.
author: consensus
context: Community discussion about which casual match bots were strongest, following elyot's investigation into matchmaking quality.

insight: According to Steel, casual match bot matchmaking was completely random rather than skill-based, and all casual bots were weaker than Master Bot. deep_drone and Masterbot2016 were considered the best of the casual bots, as they at least made semi-reasonable openings.
author: steel0229e
context: Steel disagreed with elyot's claim that matchmaking would prioritize stronger bots for stronger players, stating the system was fully random.

insight: Master Bot (and other casual AI bots) can run out of time during their turn and then continue taking their turn indefinitely, causing the game to display a 'lost contact with the prismata AI thread' error. This was reported as occurring in both casual play and Master Bot games.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 reported a recurring issue where the AI would run out of time and still keep taking its turn, with the error message persisting even after page refreshes.

insight: In the campaign puzzle 'Cryo Tactics II', a solution involving getting 4 Cryo Rays and freezing the Xeno unit only works due to bot misbehavior: the bot soaks on a Borehole Patroller instead of both shield barriers, meaning this solution would fail if the bot played correctly. The game's ending tip implies this bot-dependent solution is the intended one.
author: p0lari
context: apooche raised the issue that 'Cryo Tactics II' has two solutions, one of which only works due to bot misbehavior, and that the ending tip misleadingly implies the bot-dependent solution is intended.

insight: Exhaust 2 on a unit with a Turn ability (such as Iso Kronus) results in only 1 lost turn of that Turn ability, because the unit receives its Turn ability on the same turn it is given Exhaust 2. A click-exhaust works identically to any other exhaust mechanically.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion about how Exhaust 2 interacts with Turn abilities, prompted by a question about Iso Kronus.

insight: When a Valkyrion (or similar unit) creates a unit for the opponent without a buildtime (golden clock), and the player has already assigned overkill damage to a frontline unit, clicking Valk causes the damage to remain on the overkilled unit but prevents re-assigning that overkill until the barriers are killed. This is a UI/interaction edge case distinct from Blood Pact or Arms Race, which create opponent units with a golden clock.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 discovered and described a specific interaction where overkill assignment becomes locked after clicking a unit that creates an opponent unit without buildtime.

insight: Units created by Mobile Animus (such as Rhino) that have prompt click abilities are visually indistinguishable from existing same-turn units in some cases, causing confusion about which unit can be clicked. The clickable one has a green highlight, but community members and developers agreed this was an unintuitive UI issue worth addressing. A similar issue exists for barriers created by Asteri and units from Blood Pact and Corpus.
author: consensus
context: Multiple community members and developer elyot discussed the UI ambiguity of prompt-created units lacking a distinguishing visual indicator, with examples including Rhino from Mobile Animus and barriers from Asteri.
